description: Controlled fixed-trace matrix for testing whether class/subset wording creates transient yes/no answer basins in simple syllogisms.
question_template: All {s_plural} are {m_plural}. No {m_plural} are {o_plural}. Can any {s_singular} be {o_article} {o_singular}? Reply with exactly one line: Answer: <yes/no>.

variants.description: Original premises plus a direct valid conclusion.
Class/subset wording where the second prefix is expected to be the interference point and the final conclusion repairs it.
steps: Every {s_singular} is inside the class of {m_plural}.
The class of {m_plural} has no overlap with the class of {o_plural}.
Therefore, the class of {s_plural} has no overlap with the class of {o_plural}.

variants.description: Invalid converse-like trace inspired by rewind-side drift.
steps: If all {s_plural} are {m_plural}, then being {m_article} {m_singular} implies being {s_article} {s_singular}.
